title: Configure Circuit Breaker with DestinationRule
description:
## Configure Circuit Breaker with DestinationRule

Istio implements circuit breaking via the `outlierDetection` field in a `DestinationRule`. When a host starts returning errors, Istio ejects it from the load-balancing pool for a penalty period, protecting upstream callers.

**Your task:**

Create a `DestinationRule` named `payments` in the `istio-lab` namespace for the host `payments` with the following outlier detection settings:
- Eject a host after **3** consecutive 5xx errors (`consecutiveGatewayErrors: 3`)
- Scan for unhealthy hosts every **10s** (`interval: 10s`)
- Keep ejected hosts out for **30s** (`baseEjectionTime: 30s`)
- Allow up to **50%** of hosts to be ejected simultaneously (`maxEjectionPercent: 50`)

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get destinationrule payments -n istio-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.trafficPolicy.outlierDetection.consecutiveGatewayErrors}'
```

hints.title: DestinationRule outlierDetection structure
hints.body: Place outlierDetection under spec.trafficPolicy. All four fields must be present for full circuit breaker behaviour.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: payments
  namespace: istio-lab
spec:
  host: payments
  trafficPolicy:
    outlierDetection:
      consecutiveGatewayErrors: 3
      interval: 10s
      baseEjectionTime: 30s
      maxEjectionPercent: 50
EOF

validation.description: Check that the DestinationRule has the correct circuit breaker settings

commands.command: kubectl get destinationrule payments -n istio-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
